deck_name: The Allyship Deck — Mastering Allyship Moves

problems.label: I keep asking for help but nothing changes
I get defensive when challenged or given feedback
I'm overwhelmed — too much at once
I feel distant — I can't connect
I care but I can't seem to act
I'm running a campaign or milestone to help others

cards.title: What's Actually Scarce
cards.submovePrompt: Notice the signal. What is here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What is actually depleted here — and what only feels depleted?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this effort actually lack — money, time, hands, or hope?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the real resource gap before reacting to the felt one.
cards.remediation: Name the one resource that, if present, would change everything.
cards.flavor: Not every empty cup is the same cup.

applications.example: You feel "broke," but a closer look shows the real scarcity is time and rest, not money. Name the one resource that would change everything.
A team says it needs funding; on inspection it needs decisions. Distinguish what is depleted from what only feels depleted.

cards.title: The Number You Won't Look At
cards.submovePrompt: Notice resistance. What resists being seen?
cards.primaryQuestion: What resource truth am I refusing to look at directly?
cards.campaignQuestion: What number are we avoiding — the real cost, the real shortfall?
cards.optimizesFor: Surfacing the avoided figure so planning can be honest.
failureModes: Budgets that omit the scary line
cards.remediation: Write the exact amount needed. Look at it for ten seconds.

cards.title: What's Worth Funding
cards.submovePrompt: Notice stewardship. What deserves attention?
cards.primaryQuestion: Of all that is depleted, what actually deserves resourcing first?
cards.campaignQuestion: What in this campaign most deserves the resources we can gather?
cards.optimizesFor: Prioritizing the resource need that matters most.
cards.remediation: Rank the top three needs. Fund the first before the rest.

cards.title: The Untapped Vein
cards.submovePrompt: Notice potential. What value wants to increase?
cards.primaryQuestion: What resource is already here, underused, waiting to be grown?
cards.campaignQuestion: What asset does this campaign already have that we could multiply?
cards.optimizesFor: Spotting latent resources with upside.
cards.remediation: Name one resource you already have that could double.

cards.title: Who Holds the Purse
cards.submovePrompt: Notice relationships. What relational dynamics matter?
cards.primaryQuestion: Whose resources, trust, or permission actually shape this?
cards.campaignQuestion: Who are the givers, gatekeepers, and stakeholders in this ask?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the relational map of resource flow and power.
cards.remediation: List who must say yes for resources to move. Start there.

cards.title: The Story the Lack Tells
cards.submovePrompt: Notice meaning. What larger pattern is emerging?
cards.primaryQuestion: What pattern does this scarcity reveal about how I resource my life?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this shortfall reveal about the larger system around it?
cards.optimizesFor: Reading scarcity as a signal about a bigger pattern.
cards.remediation: Ask whether this lack has happened before. Name the pattern.

cards.title: What's Going Unseen
cards.submovePrompt: Notice the signal. What is here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What truth here is going unseen or unsaid?
cards.campaignQuestion: What in this situation is invisible that needs to become visible?
cards.optimizesFor: Noticing what's hidden or ignored before trying to amplify anything.
cards.remediation: Name one thing present in the room that no one is naming.
cards.flavor: The unseen runs the room until someone points at it.

cards.title: What Doesn't Want Light
cards.submovePrompt: Notice resistance. What resists being seen?
cards.primaryQuestion: What truth is being actively kept from view — including by me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is this system structured to keep invisible?
cards.optimizesFor: Surfacing the actively suppressed, not just the overlooked.
cards.remediation: Name the thing the room is working hard not to see.

cards.title: What Deserves the Spotlight
cards.submovePrompt: Notice stewardship. What deserves attention?
cards.primaryQuestion: Of all that could be seen, what most deserves attention right now?
cards.campaignQuestion: Whose truth or which issue most deserves visibility here?
cards.optimizesFor: Directing limited attention where it matters most.
cards.remediation: Choose the one truth worth the room's full attention.

cards.title: The Story That Could Land
cards.submovePrompt: Notice potential. What value wants to increase?
cards.primaryQuestion: What truth, if made visible, would change the most?
cards.campaignQuestion: What framing or story has the most potential to shift understanding?
cards.optimizesFor: Spotting the high-leverage truth worth amplifying.
failureModes: Missing the story that would move people
cards.remediation: Name the one fact that, if everyone saw it, would change things.

cards.title: Whose Voice Is Missing
cards.submovePrompt: Notice relationships. What relational dynamics matter?
cards.primaryQuestion: Whose perspective is absent from what's being seen?
cards.campaignQuestion: Whose voice must be centered — and who has been speaking over them?
cards.optimizesFor: Noticing the relational and power shape of attention.
cards.remediation: Name who isn't in the conversation. Bring their voice, or make room for it.

cards.title: The Pattern Beneath the Incident
cards.submovePrompt: Notice meaning. What larger pattern is emerging?
cards.primaryQuestion: What larger pattern does this single incident reveal?
cards.campaignQuestion: What systemic truth is this moment a symptom of?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the structure behind the event.
forbiddenMoves: Treating the symptom as the whole
failureModes: Framing a pattern as a one-off
cards.remediation: Connect this incident to the pattern it belongs to.

cards.title: Where the Harm Is
cards.submovePrompt: Notice the signal. What is here?
cards.primaryQuestion: Where is harm actually happening right now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What concrete harm is this action meant to confront?
cards.optimizesFor: Locating the real harm before reacting.
failureModes: Intervening where it's safe, not where harm is
cards.remediation: Name the specific harm, in specific terms.

cards.title: The Line You Won't Draw
cards.submovePrompt: Notice resistance. What resists being seen?
cards.primaryQuestion: What boundary or confrontation am I avoiding seeing the need for?
cards.campaignQuestion: What line does this situation demand that we keep not drawing?
cards.optimizesFor: Surfacing the avoided confrontation.
forbiddenMoves: Keeping the peace at the cost of harm
cards.remediation: Name the line that needs drawing — the one you've been avoiding.

cards.title: Which Fight Is Yours
cards.submovePrompt: Notice stewardship. What deserves attention?
cards.primaryQuestion: Of all the harms, which is actually mine to confront?
cards.campaignQuestion: Which intervention here is ours to take responsibility for?
cards.optimizesFor: Choosing the right fight.
failureModes: Taking on the fight that isn't yours
cards.remediation: Name the one harm you're actually positioned to confront.

cards.title: The Pressure Point
cards.submovePrompt: Notice potential. What value wants to increase?
cards.primaryQuestion: Where would action have the most effect?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the leverage point where intervention changes the most?
cards.optimizesFor: Spotting high-leverage intervention points.
forbiddenMoves: Hitting where it's visible, not where it matters
failureModes: Effort that doesn't move the harm
cards.remediation: Name the point where pressure actually shifts the outcome.

cards.title: Who's at Risk
cards.submovePrompt: Notice relationships. What relational dynamics matter?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who bears the risk if I act — me, or someone more exposed?
cards.campaignQuestion: Whose safety and power are at stake in this action?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the risk and power map of action.
failureModes: Action that endangers those it claims to protect
cards.remediation: Name who bears the risk. Don't spend others' safety.

cards.title: What the Harm Repeats
cards.submovePrompt: Notice meaning. What larger pattern is emerging?
cards.primaryQuestion: What pattern of harm is this instance part of?
cards.campaignQuestion: What systemic cycle does this action interrupt?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing action in the pattern, not just the moment.
failureModes: Addressing the instance, missing the cycle
cards.remediation: Connect this harm to the cycle it repeats, and aim there.

cards.title: What's Holding It Together
cards.submovePrompt: Notice the signal. What is here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structure — or lack of it — is actually shaping this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the real organizational state: who's doing what, and what's missing?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the actual structure before redesigning anything.
forbiddenMoves: Assuming the org chart is reality
failureModes: Missing the informal one that runs things
cards.remediation: Name how the work actually flows right now, not how it's supposed to.

cards.title: The Dysfunction No One Names
cards.submovePrompt: Notice resistance. What resists being seen?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structural problem is everyone working around but not naming?
cards.campaignQuestion: What broken process are we all quietly compensating for?
cards.optimizesFor: Surfacing the unspoken dysfunction.
cards.remediation: Name the structural problem people keep absorbing personally.

cards.title: What Needs Tending
cards.submovePrompt: Notice stewardship. What deserves attention?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which part of the structure most needs stewarding right now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What in this organization most needs tending before it breaks?
cards.optimizesFor: Directing organizing attention well.
forbiddenMoves: Tending the visible over the load-bearing
failureModes: Polishing the front while the foundation cracks
cards.remediation: Name the load-bearing part that needs care first.

cards.title: The Structure That Wants to Exist
cards.submovePrompt: Notice potential. What value wants to increase?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structure is trying to emerge that would make this work better?
cards.campaignQuestion: What system, if built, would multiply the group's capacity?
cards.optimizesFor: Spotting the high-leverage structure to build.
forbiddenMoves: Copying a template that doesn't fit
failureModes: Missing the one that would help
cards.remediation: Name the structure that would remove the most friction.

cards.title: Who Actually Decides
cards.submovePrompt: Notice relationships. What relational dynamics matter?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who holds real power and trust in how this gets organized?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the real map of roles, power, and relationship here?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the relational and power structure, not just the formal one.
failureModes: A plan that ignores who really decides
cards.remediation: Name who actually holds decision power and trust.

cards.title: The Pattern in the Chaos
cards.submovePrompt: Notice meaning. What larger pattern is emerging?
cards.primaryQuestion: What recurring organizational pattern keeps producing this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What systemic dynamic does this coordination problem reveal?
cards.optimizesFor: Seeing the pattern behind the mess.
failureModes: Fixing the instance while the pattern recurs
cards.remediation: Connect this breakdown to the pattern it repeats.

cards.title: The Empty Cup
cards.submovePrompt: Allow experience. What am I actually feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What does the lack actually feel like in my body, before I reach for a plan?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does their need actually feel like — and what dignity sits beneath the dollar amount?
cards.optimizesFor: Honest contact with the felt sense of need, so resourcing starts from truth, not panic.
forbiddenMoves: Jumping straight to a budget or plan
failureModes: Spiritual bypass ("I don't really need anything")
Talking about "the money" with no body in it
cards.remediation: Name the sensation of lack out loud — where it sits in the body — before you make a single ask.
cards.flavor: The child holding the empty cup is not a problem to solve yet. First, let yourself see the cup.

applications.example: Before drafting a fundraiser, sit with what the shortfall actually feels like — fear, shame, grief — and name it out loud before you name a number.
You need a ride to an appointment. Instead of over-explaining, notice the discomfort of needing, then make the plain ask.
A mutual-aid drive stalls. Rather than adding tactics, let the group feel what is missing — often it is rest or trust, not resources.

cards.title: The Ask You're Avoiding
cards.submovePrompt: Allow discomfort. What am I avoiding feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What resource am I afraid to ask for — and what am I avoiding feeling about needing it?
cards.campaignQuestion: What ask for them am I avoiding — and whose comfort am I protecting by not making it?
cards.optimizesFor: Contact with the avoided discomfort of dependence; surfacing the real ask under the safe one.
forbiddenMoves: Rescuing yourself just to avoid asking
Resentment in place of a request
failureModes: Fake asking (asking for rescue, not to be changed)
Saying "no" for people before they can answer
cards.remediation: Feel the "no" you're bracing against. Make the real ask anyway — for the thing you actually need.
cards.flavor: It never worked because the asking was fake. It was asking for rescue, not asking to be changed.

applications.example: You keep working weekends to avoid asking for headcount. Name the fear of looking incapable, then request the help you actually need.
You want deeper support but keep things light to avoid being a burden. Make the real ask instead of the safe one.

cards.title: Stay With the Need
cards.submovePrompt: Hold responsibility. Can I stay with this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Can I hold this need as mine to steward — without collapsing into helplessness or dumping it on someone else?
cards.campaignQuestion: Can I steward their need without making it my emergency — or quietly taking it over?
cards.optimizesFor: Staying present and responsible to the gap long enough to act from steadiness.
forbiddenMoves: Making your need someone else's emergency
Grabbing total control to feel safe
cards.remediation: Hold the need for sixty seconds without solving it. Then choose one stewarding act you can own.

cards.title: The Hidden Supply
cards.submovePrompt: Receive the resource. What energy is hidden here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What resource is already within reach that I haven't let myself receive?
cards.campaignQuestion: What resource or network for this campaign is already available that I haven't activated yet?
cards.optimizesFor: Perceiving latent and available resources — skills, relationships, slack, standing offers.
failureModes: Over-counting the future while starving the present
cards.remediation: List three resources already within reach. Receive one of them today.

cards.title: The Tenderness of Asking
cards.submovePrompt: Care for experience. How can I relate compassionately to this?
cards.primaryQuestion: How can I relate to this need — mine and others' — with care instead of shame or scorekeeping?
cards.campaignQuestion: Did they consent to this ask — and how do I invite givers without shaming the person or the givers?
cards.optimizesFor: A compassionate relational stance toward resource flow; giving and receiving without debt-shame.
cards.remediation: Offer or receive one resource with no strings — and say "no strings" out loud.

cards.title: When You Stop Fighting the Lack
cards.submovePrompt: Witness experience. What happens when I stop fighting it?
cards.primaryQuestion: What becomes clear about this need when I stop fighting it and simply witness it?
cards.campaignQuestion: When I stop forcing the campaign, what does it reveal it actually needs?
cards.optimizesFor: Integrated seeing of the resource pattern — the larger truth about what the need is really about.
cards.remediation: Watch the lack for three breaths without fixing it. Write the one true sentence that surfaces.

cards.title: What Seeing Costs
cards.submovePrompt: Allow experience. What am I actually feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What do I actually feel when I let this truth land?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does it cost us to really see what is happening here?
cards.optimizesFor: Letting the truth land in the body before reacting.
cards.remediation: Let the truth land for one breath before you respond.

cards.title: The Truth You're Numb To
cards.submovePrompt: Allow discomfort. What am I avoiding feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What truth am I keeping at arm's length because feeling it would change me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What are we avoiding feeling about this issue?
cards.optimizesFor: Contact with the truth you've gone numb to.
cards.remediation: Feel the thing you've gone numb to. Then decide from there.

cards.title: Stay Awake to It
cards.submovePrompt: Hold responsibility. Can I stay with this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Can I stay aware of this truth without looking away?
cards.campaignQuestion: Can we keep this in view long enough to act, not just react?
cards.optimizesFor: Sustained attention without collapse or burnout.
cards.remediation: Choose one truth you'll keep in view past the news cycle.

cards.title: The Energy in the Story
cards.submovePrompt: Receive the resource. What energy is hidden here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What energy or motivation is hidden in what I've seen?
cards.campaignQuestion: What latent will-to-act lives inside this awareness?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving the activating charge inside a hard truth.
failureModes: Awareness that deadens instead of activates
cards.remediation: Name the energy this truth gives you. Aim it.

cards.title: Hold the Pain With Care
cards.submovePrompt: Care for experience. How can I relate compassionately to this?
cards.primaryQuestion: How do I hold this hard truth — and others' pain in it — with care?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we relate to those harmed without spectacle or pity?
cards.optimizesFor: A compassionate stance toward painful truth.
cards.remediation: Relate to one person's reality with care, not as a case.

cards.title: When You Let It Be True
cards.submovePrompt: Witness experience. What happens when I stop fighting it?
cards.primaryQuestion: What becomes clear when I stop arguing with this truth and just witness it?
cards.campaignQuestion: What clarity comes when we stop debating and simply witness what's real?
cards.optimizesFor: Witness over debate.
cards.remediation: Witness the truth for three breaths without rebutting it.

cards.title: The Fear Under the Fight
cards.submovePrompt: Allow experience. What am I actually feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What am I actually feeling about stepping into this — fear, fury, dread?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does the team really feel about taking this risk?
cards.optimizesFor: Feeling the charge before acting on it.
failureModes: Reckless action driven by unfelt fear
cards.remediation: Feel the fear before you move. Then move anyway.

cards.title: What Action Asks of You
cards.submovePrompt: Allow discomfort. What am I avoiding feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What discomfort of confrontation am I avoiding by staying still?
cards.campaignQuestion: What are we avoiding feeling that keeps us from acting?
cards.optimizesFor: Contact with the avoided cost of acting.
cards.remediation: Feel what acting would cost. Weigh it against the cost of not acting.

cards.title: Can You Hold the Line
cards.submovePrompt: Hold responsibility. Can I stay with this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Can I stay in this confrontation without collapsing or escalating?
cards.campaignQuestion: Can we sustain this action without burning out or losing control?
cards.optimizesFor: Staying steady inside conflict.
cards.remediation: Stay one beat longer in the discomfort than feels possible.

cards.title: The Power in the Anger
cards.submovePrompt: Receive the resource. What energy is hidden here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What usable power is hidden in this anger or urgency?
cards.campaignQuestion: What force does this moment's energy give the action?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving anger as fuel for clean action.
failureModes: Anger that burns the actor, not the harm
cards.remediation: Name the agency this anger gives you. Point it at the harm.

cards.title: Fierce and Caring
cards.submovePrompt: Care for experience. How can I relate compassionately to this?
cards.primaryQuestion: How do I confront harm while still relating to people with care?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we act fiercely without dehumanizing even those we oppose?
cards.optimizesFor: Combining fierceness with care.
cards.remediation: Name the boundary fiercely; keep one thread of care for the human.

cards.title: See Before You Strike
cards.submovePrompt: Witness experience. What happens when I stop fighting it?
cards.primaryQuestion: What do I see clearly if I pause and witness before reacting?
cards.campaignQuestion: What clarity comes if we witness the full picture before intervening?
cards.optimizesFor: Witness that sharpens action rather than delaying it.
cards.remediation: Witness the whole field for three breaths, then act with aim.

cards.title: What the Mess Feels Like
cards.submovePrompt: Allow experience. What am I actually feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What do I actually feel inside this disorganization — overwhelm, resentment, dread?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the felt experience of being in this structure, or its absence?
cards.optimizesFor: Feeling the human cost of the structure before fixing it.
failureModes: Process fixes that ignore how it feels to live in them
cards.remediation: Name what the current structure feels like to be inside.

cards.title: The Role You're Avoiding
cards.submovePrompt: Allow discomfort. What am I avoiding feeling?
cards.primaryQuestion: What organizing responsibility am I avoiding because it's uncomfortable?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structural decision are we avoiding because it'll upset someone?
cards.optimizesFor: Contact with the avoided organizing work.
forbiddenMoves: Avoiding the hard role or delegation talk
failureModes: Structure left vague to avoid discomfort
cards.remediation: Name the role or decision you're avoiding.

cards.title: Can You Hold the Whole
cards.submovePrompt: Hold responsibility. Can I stay with this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Can I hold responsibility for this structure without controlling everything or dropping it?
cards.campaignQuestion: Can we steward this organization steadily, sharing the load?
cards.optimizesFor: Holding the whole without over-controlling or abdicating.
failureModes: The structure depending on one exhausted person
cards.remediation: Hold the whole for a beat; then hand one piece to someone you trust.

cards.title: The Capacity in the Room
cards.submovePrompt: Receive the resource. What energy is hidden here?
cards.primaryQuestion: What organizing capacity or willingness is here, unused?
cards.campaignQuestion: What latent capacity in this group hasn't been activated?
cards.optimizesFor: Receiving the group's untapped capacity.
failureModes: Scarcity of help while help stands unused
cards.remediation: Name three people's capacities you haven't drawn on. Invite one.

cards.title: Structure That Cares
cards.submovePrompt: Care for experience. How can I relate compassionately to this?
cards.primaryQuestion: How do I build structure that holds people with care, not just efficiency?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we organize in a way that tends relationships, not just tasks?
cards.optimizesFor: Humane structure.
failureModes: A system that works and burns people
cards.remediation: Design one part of the structure around how it cares for people.

cards.title: When You Stop Forcing Order
cards.submovePrompt: Witness experience. What happens when I stop fighting it?
cards.primaryQuestion: What becomes clear when I stop forcing order and witness how the work wants to flow?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structure reveals itself when we stop imposing and start watching?
cards.optimizesFor: Witnessing the natural flow before imposing structure.
failureModes: Rigid structure fighting the actual work
cards.remediation: Watch how the work actually wants to move for three breaths. Organize with that.

cards.title: Name the Money Feeling
cards.submovePrompt: Identify channel. Which channel is active — Fire, Water, Metal, Earth, or Wood?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel is live around this resource — fear, anger, sadness, numbness, or reach?
cards.campaignQuestion: What emotional channel is driving how we're resourcing this — and is it clean?
cards.optimizesFor: Locating the active channel so the charge can move.
forbiddenMoves: Acting on the charge before naming it
cards.remediation: Name the channel out loud: "This is fear / anger / sadness about money."

cards.title: The Money Story
cards.submovePrompt: Challenge interpretation. What story am I believing?
cards.primaryQuestion: What story about deserving, scarcity, or worth am I treating as fact?
cards.campaignQuestion: What limiting story — "we can't ask for that much" — are we believing?
cards.optimizesFor: Exposing the inherited resource script.
cards.remediation: Write the story as a sentence. Then write the version that isn't certain.
cards.flavor: Inherited scripts run the budget until you read them aloud.

cards.title: The Capability You're Missing
cards.submovePrompt: Identify missing move. What capability is unavailable?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which capability is offline — can I not act, connect, explore, rest, or participate around this?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capability does this campaign lack — agency to ask, connection to mobilize?
cards.optimizesFor: Naming the missing capability behind the resource block.
forbiddenMoves: Adding tactics when the block is a missing capability
failureModes: Doing more of a move you can't yet make
cards.remediation: Match the block to a capability. That's the move to grow next.

cards.title: Move the Charge
cards.submovePrompt: Select transformation. Transcend, Translate, or Neutralize?
cards.primaryQuestion: Do I transcend this feeling, translate it to another channel, or neutralize it?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we convert this campaign's charge into usable fuel — transcend, translate, neutralize?
cards.optimizesFor: Choosing the alchemical operation that frees resourcing.
cards.remediation: Pick one: transcend (let it ripen), translate (e.g. fear → agency), or neutralize. Then do it.

cards.title: Fear Into Invitation
cards.submovePrompt: Choose destination. Which channel would better serve this situation?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel, if I moved this charge there, would serve the ask better?
cards.campaignQuestion: What would the ask feel like if it came from connection instead of fear?
cards.optimizesFor: Aiming the charge toward a relationally generative channel.
cards.remediation: Re-aim the ask from Connection: invite people into something good.

cards.title: What the Shortfall Taught
cards.submovePrompt: Extract insight. What does this teach me?
cards.primaryQuestion: What does this resource struggle teach me that I can keep?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the durable lesson this campaign's resourcing is offering?
cards.optimizesFor: Converting the struggle into reusable insight (a BAR).
failureModes: Repeating the lesson because it wasn't extracted
cards.remediation: Write one sentence you'll carry into the next ask.

cards.title: Name the Awareness Feeling
cards.submovePrompt: Identify channel. Which channel is active — Fire, Water, Metal, Earth, or Wood?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel is live in this awareness — anger, grief, fear, numbness, or reach?
cards.campaignQuestion: What emotional channel is driving how we raise this — and is it clean?
cards.optimizesFor: Locating the channel under the message.
cards.remediation: Name the channel before you publish a single word.

cards.title: The Story About the Truth
cards.submovePrompt: Challenge interpretation. What story am I believing?
cards.primaryQuestion: What story am I telling about what I saw that may not be the whole truth?
cards.campaignQuestion: What narrative are we attached to that the facts might complicate?
cards.optimizesFor: Challenging your own framing before broadcasting it.
failureModes: Truth bent to fit the story
cards.remediation: Write the version of events that complicates your story.

cards.title: What Awareness Lacks
cards.submovePrompt: Identify missing move. What capability is unavailable?
cards.primaryQuestion: Awareness alone isn't moving me — which capability is missing?
cards.campaignQuestion: We've raised awareness but nothing shifts — what capability is the gap?
cards.optimizesFor: Naming the missing capability behind stalled awareness.
forbiddenMoves: More awareness as the answer to everything
cards.remediation: Name the capability awareness needs next — to act, connect, or organize.

cards.title: From Outrage to Aim
cards.submovePrompt: Select transformation. Transcend, Translate, or Neutralize?
cards.primaryQuestion: Do I transcend this charge, translate it (anger to clarity), or neutralize it before I speak?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we convert this issue's charge into communicable clarity?
cards.optimizesFor: Choosing the alchemy that makes truth land.
cards.remediation: Pick transcend / translate / neutralize, then craft the message.

cards.title: The Tone That Reaches
cards.submovePrompt: Choose destination. Which channel would better serve this situation?
cards.primaryQuestion: What emotional tone would let this truth actually reach people?
cards.campaignQuestion: From which channel would this message invite rather than alienate?
cards.optimizesFor: Aiming tone for reception, not just release.
cards.remediation: Re-aim the message from a channel that opens people, not closes them.

cards.title: What the Truth Taught
cards.submovePrompt: Extract insight. What does this teach me?
cards.primaryQuestion: What durable insight do I take from sitting with this truth?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the lasting lesson this awareness work is offering us?
cards.optimizesFor: Converting awareness into reusable wisdom.
forbiddenMoves: The hot take over the insight
cards.remediation: Write the one insight you'll keep, beyond this moment.

cards.title: Name the Fight Feeling
cards.submovePrompt: Identify channel. Which channel is active — Fire, Water, Metal, Earth, or Wood?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel is driving this — clean fire, or fear or grief wearing fire's mask?
cards.campaignQuestion: What channel is fueling this action, and is it clean?
cards.optimizesFor: Locating the true channel under the urge to act.
cards.remediation: Name the channel honestly before you intervene.

cards.title: The Enemy Story
cards.submovePrompt: Challenge interpretation. What story am I believing?
cards.primaryQuestion: What story about who's the enemy am I treating as fact?
cards.campaignQuestion: What us-versus-them narrative might be distorting our read?
cards.optimizesFor: Challenging the framing that justifies the action.
failureModes: Action built on a distorted enemy
cards.remediation: Write the version where the "enemy" is more complicated. Then aim true.

cards.title: What the Fight Needs
cards.submovePrompt: Identify missing move. What capability is unavailable?
cards.primaryQuestion: I'm stuck on this confrontation — which capability is missing?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capability does this action lack — agency, connection, structure?
cards.optimizesFor: Naming the missing capability behind stalled action.
failureModes: Pushing harder on a missing capability
cards.remediation: Name the capability the action needs next, and grow it.

cards.title: Forge the Anger
cards.submovePrompt: Select transformation. Transcend, Translate, or Neutralize?
cards.primaryQuestion: Do I transcend this charge, translate it (fear to agency), or neutralize it before acting?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we forge this charge into clean, aimed force?
cards.optimizesFor: Alchemizing charge into clean action.
cards.remediation: Pick transcend / translate / neutralize, then act from the forged state.
cards.flavor: Forge anger into a boundary said, a limit set.

cards.title: From Rage to Resolve
cards.submovePrompt: Choose destination. Which channel would better serve this situation?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel, if I moved this charge there, would make the action cleaner?
cards.campaignQuestion: What would this action look like from resolve instead of rage?
cards.optimizesFor: Aiming the charge toward effective action.
cards.remediation: Re-aim from resolve — an earned boundary, not blind heat.

cards.title: What the Confrontation Taught
cards.submovePrompt: Extract insight. What does this teach me?
cards.primaryQuestion: What do I learn from this fight that I'll carry forward?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the durable lesson this action is teaching us?
cards.optimizesFor: Converting confrontation into wisdom.
cards.remediation: Write the one lesson this confrontation taught you.

cards.title: Name the Organizing Feeling
cards.submovePrompt: Identify channel. Which channel is active — Fire, Water, Metal, Earth, or Wood?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel is live in this coordination strain — fear, anger, overwhelm, numbness?
cards.campaignQuestion: What emotional channel is driving our organizing, and is it clean?
cards.optimizesFor: Locating the channel under the process strain.
cards.remediation: Name the channel before you redesign anything.

cards.title: The 'Only I Can' Story
cards.submovePrompt: Challenge interpretation. What story am I believing?
cards.primaryQuestion: What story about who can do this am I treating as fact?
cards.campaignQuestion: What belief — "only we can," "they'll mess it up" — is blocking delegation?
cards.optimizesFor: Challenging the story that prevents sharing the work.
cards.remediation: Write the story that keeps you the bottleneck. Then test it: hand one thing off.

cards.title: What the System Lacks
cards.submovePrompt: Identify missing move. What capability is unavailable?
cards.primaryQuestion: This keeps breaking — which capability is the structure missing?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capability does this organization lack — clarity, trust, follow-through?
cards.optimizesFor: Naming the missing capability behind the breakdown.
forbiddenMoves: Adding process to a capability gap
cards.remediation: Name the capability the structure needs — not another rule.

cards.title: Redirect the Strain
cards.submovePrompt: Select transformation. Transcend, Translate, or Neutralize?
cards.primaryQuestion: Do I transcend this strain, translate it, or neutralize it before I (re)build?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we convert organizing strain into clean structure?
cards.optimizesFor: Clearing the charge before designing.
cards.remediation: Clear the charge first; then design from steadiness.

cards.title: From Control to Trust
cards.submovePrompt: Choose destination. Which channel would better serve this situation?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which channel would let me organize from trust instead of control?
cards.campaignQuestion: What would this structure look like built from trust, not fear?
cards.optimizesFor: Aiming organizing energy toward trust.
cards.remediation: Re-aim one structure from trust — give real ownership, not just tasks.

cards.title: What the Breakdown Taught
cards.submovePrompt: Extract insight. What does this teach me?
cards.primaryQuestion: What does this organizing breakdown teach me to build differently?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the durable lesson this coordination struggle offers?
cards.optimizesFor: Converting breakdown into design wisdom.
cards.remediation: Write the one design principle this breakdown taught you.

cards.title: The Capacity Trying to Grow
cards.submovePrompt: Identify emerging capacity. What wants to grow?
cards.primaryQuestion: What resourcing capacity in me is trying to grow right now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capability is this campaign asking us to develop?
cards.optimizesFor: Noticing the growth edge the resource work is surfacing.
failureModes: Naming a capacity you wish for over the one actually emerging
cards.remediation: Name the capacity one size bigger than today. Practice it once.

cards.title: The Edge of the Ask
cards.submovePrompt: Identify developmental edge. What must evolve?
cards.primaryQuestion: What must evolve in me to ask or steward at the next level?
cards.campaignQuestion: What must the team learn to resource this at the scale it needs?
cards.optimizesFor: Locating the precise developmental edge.
forbiddenMoves: Comfortable practice that isn't the edge
failureModes: Training the strength, avoiding the edge
cards.remediation: Name the thing that scares you slightly. That's the edge — step to it.

cards.title: Worth Practicing
cards.submovePrompt: Steward growth. What deserves practice?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which resourcing skill deserves deliberate, repeated practice?
cards.campaignQuestion: What practice should this campaign repeat until it is reliable?
cards.optimizesFor: Committing to repeatable practice over one-off effort.
cards.remediation: Choose one rep ("make one real ask weekly"). Schedule it.

cards.title: Strengthen the Channel
cards.submovePrompt: Amplify capacity. What capability wants strengthening?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which existing capability, if strengthened, would unlock resourcing?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity, amplified, would most increase what we can gather?
cards.optimizesFor: Investing growth where it compounds.
failureModes: Polishing a capability you don't need
cards.remediation: Pick the capability with the highest leverage. Train it deliberately.

cards.title: Growing Without Leaving People
cards.submovePrompt: Relate growth. How does this affect others?
cards.primaryQuestion: As I grow my capacity to resource, how does it land on the people around me?
cards.campaignQuestion: How does scaling this campaign affect the relationships that carry it?
cards.optimizesFor: Growing in a way that strengthens, not strains, relationships.
cards.remediation: Name one person your growth affects. Bring them with you.

cards.title: Who Resourcing Is Making Me
cards.submovePrompt: Integrate growth. Who am I becoming?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who am I becoming through learning to gather and steward resources?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is this campaign making us, as a community, become?
cards.optimizesFor: Integrating the identity shift the work is producing.
cards.remediation: Finish the sentence: "I am becoming someone who ______."

cards.title: The Witness Growing in You
cards.submovePrompt: Identify emerging capacity. What wants to grow?
cards.primaryQuestion: What capacity to see or speak truth is trying to grow in me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What awareness-capacity is this work asking the group to develop?
cards.optimizesFor: Noticing the emerging witness or voice.
failureModes: Naming the capacity you wish for over the one emerging
cards.remediation: Name the seeing or speaking capacity one size bigger than today.

cards.title: The Edge of Speaking
cards.submovePrompt: Identify developmental edge. What must evolve?
cards.primaryQuestion: What must evolve in me to name this truth out loud?
cards.campaignQuestion: What must we learn to tell this truth at the scale it needs?
cards.optimizesFor: Finding the truth-telling edge.
forbiddenMoves: Safe statements that aren't the edge
failureModes: Training comfortable speech, avoiding the edge
cards.remediation: Name the sentence you're scared to say. That's the edge.

cards.title: Practice the Hard Conversation
cards.submovePrompt: Steward growth. What deserves practice?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which awareness skill — listening, naming, calling in — deserves deliberate practice?
cards.campaignQuestion: What communication practice should we repeat until it's reliable?
cards.optimizesFor: Committing to a repeatable practice over a single brave moment.
cards.remediation: Pick one rep — a weekly hard conversation. Schedule it.

cards.title: Sharpen the Signal
cards.submovePrompt: Amplify capacity. What capability wants strengthening?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which communication capability, if strengthened, would make my truth land better?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity, amplified, would most increase our reach?
cards.optimizesFor: Investing growth where reach compounds.
cards.remediation: Pick the highest-leverage skill — clarity, story, or listening. Train it.

cards.title: Speaking Without Burning
cards.submovePrompt: Relate growth. How does this affect others?
cards.primaryQuestion: As I get bolder with truth, how does it land on the people around me?
cards.campaignQuestion: How does our louder voice affect allies, targets, and the harmed?
cards.optimizesFor: Growing voice without scorching relationships.
cards.remediation: Name one relationship your bolder voice affects. Tend it.

cards.title: Who Truth-Telling Makes You
cards.submovePrompt: Integrate growth. Who am I becoming?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who am I becoming as someone who sees and names what's real?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is this awareness work making us, as a community, become?
cards.optimizesFor: Integrating the identity of a truth-teller.
cards.remediation: Finish the sentence: "I am becoming someone who can see and say ______."

cards.title: The Courage Trying to Grow
cards.submovePrompt: Identify emerging capacity. What wants to grow?
cards.primaryQuestion: What capacity for action or boundary is trying to grow in me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What action-capacity is this work asking us to develop?
cards.optimizesFor: Noticing the emerging courage.
failureModes: Naming the courage you wish for over the one emerging
cards.remediation: Name the boundary or action one size bigger than today. Take it once.

cards.title: The Edge of the Line
cards.submovePrompt: Identify developmental edge. What must evolve?
cards.primaryQuestion: What must evolve in me to draw the line this calls for?
cards.campaignQuestion: What must we learn to confront at the scale this needs?
cards.optimizesFor: Finding the action edge.
forbiddenMoves: Safe action that isn't the edge
failureModes: Training comfort, avoiding the real confrontation
cards.remediation: Name the confrontation that scares you slightly. Step to it.

cards.title: Practice Holding the Line
cards.submovePrompt: Steward growth. What deserves practice?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which action skill — saying no, staying steady, de-escalating — deserves practice?
cards.campaignQuestion: What action practice should we drill until it's reliable?
cards.optimizesFor: Repeatable practice of action skills.
cards.remediation: Pick one rep — a small "no" daily. Practice it.

cards.title: Strengthen Your Stance
cards.submovePrompt: Amplify capacity. What capability wants strengthening?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which existing capability, if strengthened, would make my action more effective?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity, amplified, would most increase our impact?
cards.optimizesFor: Investing growth where impact compounds.
cards.remediation: Pick the highest-leverage action capability. Train it.

cards.title: Acting Without Abandoning
cards.submovePrompt: Relate growth. How does this affect others?
cards.primaryQuestion: As I get bolder in action, how does it land on those beside me?
cards.campaignQuestion: How does escalating this action affect allies and the people we protect?
cards.optimizesFor: Growing action without endangering or alienating allies.
failureModes: Bold action that isolates or endangers
cards.remediation: Name who your bolder action affects. Move with them, not past them.

cards.title: Who the Fight Makes You
cards.submovePrompt: Integrate growth. Who am I becoming?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who am I becoming as someone willing to act and confront?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is this struggle making us, as a community, become?
cards.optimizesFor: Integrating the identity of one who acts.
cards.remediation: Finish the sentence: "I am becoming someone who can act and still ______."

cards.title: The Organizer Growing in You
cards.submovePrompt: Identify emerging capacity. What wants to grow?
cards.primaryQuestion: What organizing or coordinating capacity is trying to grow in me?
cards.campaignQuestion: What organizing capacity is this work asking the group to develop?
cards.optimizesFor: Noticing the emerging organizer.
failureModes: Naming the capacity you wish for over the one emerging
cards.remediation: Name the organizing capacity one size bigger than today. Practice it once.

cards.title: The Edge of Letting Go
cards.submovePrompt: Identify developmental edge. What must evolve?
cards.primaryQuestion: What must evolve in me to organize at the next level — often, to let go?
cards.campaignQuestion: What must the group learn to coordinate at the scale it needs?
cards.optimizesFor: Finding the organizing edge — often delegation and trust.
forbiddenMoves: Comfortable control that isn't the edge
cards.remediation: Name the thing you must stop doing yourself. Hand it off.

cards.title: Practice Delegating
cards.submovePrompt: Steward growth. What deserves practice?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which organizing skill — delegating, facilitating, documenting — deserves practice?
cards.campaignQuestion: What organizing practice should we repeat until it's reliable?
cards.optimizesFor: Repeatable practice of organizing skills.
forbiddenMoves: Hoping coordination improves on its own
cards.remediation: Pick one rep — delegate one real thing weekly. Practice it.

cards.title: Strengthen the System
cards.submovePrompt: Amplify capacity. What capability wants strengthening?
cards.primaryQuestion: Which capability, if strengthened, would make the whole structure more effective?
cards.campaignQuestion: What capacity, amplified, would most increase the group's throughput?
cards.optimizesFor: Investing where structural leverage compounds.
cards.remediation: Pick the highest-leverage capability — clarity, trust, or follow-through. Build it.

cards.title: Growing the Team, Not Just You
cards.submovePrompt: Relate growth. How does this affect others?
cards.primaryQuestion: As I grow as an organizer, am I growing others' capacity too?
cards.campaignQuestion: How does scaling our structure develop people, not just exploit them?
cards.optimizesFor: Growing capacity in a way that develops others.
failureModes: One strong organizer, a dependent group
cards.remediation: Name one person whose capacity your growth could grow. Invest in them.

cards.title: Who Organizing Makes You
cards.submovePrompt: Integrate growth. Who am I becoming?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who am I becoming as someone who builds and holds structure?
cards.campaignQuestion: What is this organizing work making us, as a community, become?
cards.optimizesFor: Integrating the identity of a builder and steward.
cards.remediation: Finish the sentence: "I am becoming someone who can build and still ______."

cards.title: Aim the Resources
cards.submovePrompt: Choose domain. Where will I invest this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Where exactly will I invest what I've gathered?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where will this campaign's resources actually go — concretely?
cards.optimizesFor: Committing gathered resources to a specific target.
cards.remediation: Name the first concrete place the resources go this week.

cards.title: The Ask Goes Live
cards.submovePrompt: Create intervention. What must change?
cards.primaryQuestion: What concrete resourcing act must I take now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What intervention launches the gather — the page, the ask, the event?
cards.optimizesFor: Shipping the actual ask or intervention.
cards.remediation: Post the ask today. Make it real and specific.
cards.flavor: A proposal names the next step when no one else will.

cards.title: Hold the Funds Well
cards.submovePrompt: Create stewardship. What deserves ongoing support?
cards.primaryQuestion: What ongoing stewardship will keep these resources trustworthy?
cards.campaignQuestion: How will we steward and account for what is given — transparently?
cards.optimizesFor: Building accountable stewardship of gathered resources.
cards.remediation: Set one transparency practice (a public update, a ledger). Keep it.

cards.title: Build the Ladder
cards.submovePrompt: Create structure. What value am I amplifying?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structure makes the resourcing repeatable and growing?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structure — milestones, tiers, matches — amplifies this campaign?
cards.optimizesFor: Creating structure that compounds resource flow.
cards.remediation: Add one structural lever: a milestone ladder ($200 / $400 / $800) or a match.

cards.title: Invite, Don't Extract
cards.submovePrompt: Create relationship. Who must be involved? What power dynamic must be addressed?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who must be invited, and what power dynamic must I name to do this cleanly?
cards.campaignQuestion: Who do we invite to give — and whose consent and power must we honor?
cards.optimizesFor: Resourcing through invitation and honored consent, not extraction.
cards.remediation: Invite one person with full consent and no strings. Name the dynamic aloud.

cards.title: What Remains After
cards.submovePrompt: Create legacy. What artifact remains?
cards.primaryQuestion: What lasting artifact or story will remain from this resourcing?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this campaign leave behind — capacity, relationships, a story?
cards.optimizesFor: Ensuring the effort leaves durable value beyond the dollars.
cards.remediation: Name the artifact that outlives the campaign. Tell its story to one giver.

cards.title: Aim the Awareness
cards.submovePrompt: Choose domain. Where will I invest this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Where exactly will I direct this awareness into the world?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where will this campaign's message actually go — which audience, which channel?
cards.optimizesFor: Committing awareness to a specific target.
cards.remediation: Name the one audience and channel this truth goes to first.

cards.title: Say the Thing
cards.submovePrompt: Create intervention. What must change?
cards.primaryQuestion: What truth must I actually state — publicly or to someone's face — now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the intervention: the post, the testimony, the call-out or call-in?
cards.optimizesFor: Shipping the actual act of witness.
forbiddenMoves: A soft hint instead of the thing
failureModes: The truth that never gets said
cards.remediation: Say the thing today — specifically, to the people who need to hear it.
cards.flavor: The naming is the first move.

applications.example: A colleague is talked over repeatedly. Instead of a vague follow-up, say it in the room: “I want to hear the rest of what she was saying.”
You have drafted the post three times. Publish the specific truth today, addressed to the people who need to hear it.

cards.title: Keep the Truth Alive
cards.submovePrompt: Create stewardship. What deserves ongoing support?
cards.primaryQuestion: What ongoing practice keeps this truth in view after the moment passes?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we steward this awareness so it doesn't fade with the news cycle?
cards.optimizesFor: Building durable, accountable attention.
cards.remediation: Set one recurring act that keeps the truth present.

cards.title: Build the Megaphone
cards.submovePrompt: Create structure. What value am I amplifying?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structure makes this truth travel further and repeat?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structure — a series, toolkit, or platform — amplifies the message?
cards.optimizesFor: Structure that compounds reach.
failureModes: A message that can't scale or repeat
cards.remediation: Add one structural lever: a series, a shareable artifact, or a coalition list.

cards.title: Amplify, Don't Speak Over
cards.submovePrompt: Create relationship. Who must be involved? What power dynamic must be addressed?
cards.primaryQuestion: Whose voice should I amplify, and what power dynamic must I name to do it right?
cards.campaignQuestion: Who must lead this message, and whose consent and centering matter?
cards.optimizesFor: Raising awareness by centering the affected, not yourself.
cards.remediation: Amplify one affected voice with consent; name your own role honestly.

cards.title: What the Telling Leaves
cards.submovePrompt: Create legacy. What artifact remains?
cards.primaryQuestion: What lasting artifact of this truth will remain — a record, a story, a shift?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this awareness campaign leave behind beyond the moment?
cards.optimizesFor: A durable record or shift beyond the attention spike.
cards.remediation: Name the artifact — the documented account, the changed mind — that outlives the post.

cards.title: Aim the Action
cards.submovePrompt: Choose domain. Where will I invest this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Where exactly will I direct this action?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where will this action land — which target, which moment?
cards.optimizesFor: Committing action to a specific target.
cards.remediation: Name the one place this action lands first.

cards.title: Make the Move
cards.submovePrompt: Create intervention. What must change?
cards.primaryQuestion: What concrete confrontation or intervention must I make now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the action that actually goes — the stand, the disruption, the demand?
cards.optimizesFor: Shipping the actual intervention.
forbiddenMoves: A hint instead of the act
cards.remediation: Take the action today — specific, real, visible.
cards.flavor: A challenge opens a new front.

cards.title: Sustain the Pressure
cards.submovePrompt: Create stewardship. What deserves ongoing support?
cards.primaryQuestion: What ongoing commitment keeps this action from being a one-off?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we sustain and steward this action over time?
cards.optimizesFor: Durable, accountable pressure.
cards.remediation: Set one sustainable recurring commitment to keep the pressure on.

cards.title: Build the Leverage
cards.submovePrompt: Create structure. What value am I amplifying?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structure makes this action repeatable and harder to ignore?
cards.campaignQuestion: What structure — escalation ladder, tactics, roles — amplifies the action?
cards.optimizesFor: Structure that compounds pressure.
failureModes: Action that can't escalate or repeat
cards.remediation: Add one structural lever — an escalation step or a clear next demand.

cards.title: Act With, Not For
cards.submovePrompt: Create relationship. Who must be involved? What power dynamic must be addressed?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who must be involved, and what power dynamic must I name to act cleanly?
cards.campaignQuestion: Who leads this action, whose consent matters, and who must be protected?
cards.optimizesFor: Action rooted in consent and shared power, not saviorism.
cards.remediation: Act with the affected, by their lead; name and protect who's at risk.

cards.title: What the Action Leaves
cards.submovePrompt: Create legacy. What artifact remains?
cards.primaryQuestion: What lasting change or precedent will this action leave?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this action leave behind — a precedent, a protection, a shift?
cards.optimizesFor: Durable change beyond the moment of confrontation.
cards.remediation: Name the change that must outlast the action; secure it.

cards.title: Aim the Structure
cards.submovePrompt: Choose domain. Where will I invest this?
cards.primaryQuestion: Where exactly will I invest this organizing energy?
cards.campaignQuestion: Where will this structure actually apply — which process, which team?
cards.optimizesFor: Committing organizing to a specific target.
failureModes: Systems built for nothing in particular
cards.remediation: Name the one process this structure serves first.

cards.title: Ship the System
cards.submovePrompt: Create intervention. What must change?
cards.primaryQuestion: What concrete structural change must I make now?
cards.campaignQuestion: What's the intervention — the role defined, the process launched, the meeting fixed?
cards.optimizesFor: Shipping the actual structural change.
forbiddenMoves: A suggestion instead of a change
cards.remediation: Make the structural change today — define one role, fix one process.

cards.title: Tend the Structure
cards.submovePrompt: Create stewardship. What deserves ongoing support?
cards.primaryQuestion: What ongoing stewardship keeps this structure healthy over time?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we maintain and steward this system so it doesn't rot?
cards.optimizesFor: Durable, accountable maintenance.
cards.remediation: Assign one owner and one review rhythm to the structure.

cards.title: Build to Last
cards.submovePrompt: Create structure. What value am I amplifying?
cards.primaryQuestion: What structure makes this repeatable, scalable, and resilient?
cards.campaignQuestion: What system — roles, process, handoffs — amplifies the group's work?
cards.optimizesFor: Structure that compounds capacity.
failureModes: A structure that can't scale or survive a departure
cards.remediation: Add one durable lever — a documented process, a clear role, a handoff.
cards.flavor: Design is making complexity navigable without making it simple.

cards.title: Share the Power
cards.submovePrompt: Create relationship. Who must be involved? What power dynamic must be addressed?
cards.primaryQuestion: Who must hold real ownership, and what power dynamic must I name to build this fairly?
cards.campaignQuestion: How do we distribute roles and power so the structure is just, not concentrated?
cards.optimizesFor: Structure that shares power and consent, rather than centralizing it.
failureModes: A structure that entrenches the few
cards.remediation: Distribute one piece of real ownership; name the power dynamic openly.

cards.title: What the Structure Leaves
cards.submovePrompt: Create legacy. What artifact remains?
cards.primaryQuestion: What lasting capacity or institution will this organizing leave behind?
cards.campaignQuestion: What does this organizing leave behind — a durable structure, trained people?
cards.optimizesFor: Durable institutional capacity beyond the effort.
failureModes: The work collapses when you leave
cards.remediation: Name the structure or trained people that outlast you; secure them.

cards.title: What this deck is
cards.body: A spellbook of allyship moves. Draw a card for inspiration, or consult deliberately to find the move that restores a capability you have lost access to.

cards.title: How to draw
cards.body: Shuffle and reveal one card. Read its question. Do its remediation — the small practice at the bottom. That is the whole move.

cards.title: How to consult
cards.body: Name your situation. Find the matching problem, or the capability that feels offline, and follow it to the cards that restore it.

cards.title: Allyship for self vs others
cards.body: Every move card reads two ways. Allyship for self: use the first question for your own inner work. Allyship for others: use the campaign question for a milestone, campaign, or relationship in service of someone else.

cards.title: Reading a card
cards.body: A card names an Operation (how you act), a Move (what kind of progress), and a Domain (the allyship context). The question opens it; the remediation closes it.

cards.title: The BAR flow
cards.body: The loop: a Charge moves through Wake Up (Awareness) → Open Up (Experience) → Clean Up (Insight) → Grow Up (Capacity) → Show Up (Artifact). Each move makes a BAR.

cards.title: You are restoring capability
cards.body: You are never trying to eliminate emotion. Every stuck feeling is a capability gone offline. The move restores access.

cards.title: Move: Wake Up
cards.body: Detect charge. Ask: "What is happening?" Output: a awareness BAR.

cards.title: Move: Open Up
cards.body: Receive charge. Ask: "What energy is trying to get through?" Output: a experience BAR.

cards.title: Move: Clean Up
cards.body: Transform charge. Ask: "What move is missing?" Output: a insight BAR.

cards.title: Move: Grow Up
cards.body: Develop capability. Ask: "Who must I become?" Output: a wisdom BAR.

cards.title: Move: Show Up
cards.body: Invest capacity. Ask: "What shall I create?" Output: a artifact BAR.

cards.title: Operation: Shaman
cards.body: Notice. Ask: "What is here?" Every move passes through this operation — the faces are operations, not classes.

cards.title: Operation: Challenger
cards.body: Challenge. Ask: "What resists?" Every move passes through this operation — the faces are operations, not classes.

cards.title: Operation: Regent
cards.body: Steward. Ask: "What deserves responsibility?" Every move passes through this operation — the faces are operations, not classes.

cards.title: Operation: Architect
cards.body: Amplify. Ask: "What value wants to be increased?" Every move passes through this operation — the faces are operations, not classes.

cards.title: Operation: Diplomat
cards.body: Care. Ask: "What relationships or power dynamics matter?" Every move passes through this operation — the faces are operations, not classes.

cards.title: Operation: Sage
cards.body: Integrate. Ask: "What larger truth is emerging?" Every move passes through this operation — the faces are operations, not classes.

cards.title: Domain: Gather Resources
cards.body: The allyship context of need, asking, and marshaling resources.

cards.title: Domain: Raise Awareness
cards.body: The allyship context of attention, truth, and what must become visible.

cards.title: Domain: Direct Action
cards.body: The allyship context of the line, intervention, and what must change.

cards.title: Domain: Skillful Organizing
cards.body: The allyship context of structure, coordination, and who does what.

cards.title: Capability: Agency (Fire → Triumph)
cards.body: When Fire resolves from Anger to Triumph, you restore: "I can act."

cards.title: Capability: Connection (Water → Poignance)
cards.body: When Water resolves from Sadness to Poignance, you restore: "I can connect."

cards.title: Capability: Exploration (Metal → Wonder)
cards.body: When Metal resolves from Fear to Wonder, you restore: "I can explore."

cards.title: Capability: Rest (Earth → Peace)
cards.body: When Earth resolves from Neutrality to Peace, you restore: "I can rest."

cards.title: Capability: Participation (Wood → Bliss)
cards.body: When Wood resolves from Joy (stuck) to Bliss, you restore: "I can participate."
